text: Iliotibial band syndrome constitutes approximately 10% of all running-related injuries.
Hip abductor strengthening exercises are a consistently recommended conservative treatment strategy for iliotibial band syndrome in runners.
Conservative treatment for ITBS can achieve pain reduction ranging from 27% to 100% over 2 to 8 weeks of intervention.
Conservative treatment for ITBS can achieve functional improvement ranging from 10% to 57% over 2 to 8 weeks of intervention.
Augmenting hip abductor strengthening with shockwave therapy or manual therapy may enhance conservative treatment outcomes for ITBS.
Gait retraining is an emerging strategy for ITBS management that requires further rigorous investigation.
